# Daily AI dev briefing — 2026-03-28

## News

- **GitHub will train on Copilot interaction data by default — opt out by April 24** — https://github.blog/changelog/2026-03-25-updates-to-our-privacy-statement-and-terms-of-service-how-we-use-your-data/ — Updated ToS effective April 24: inputs, outputs, code snippets, and context from Copilot Free/Pro/Pro+ sessions used for AI training unless opted out. Not raw private repo contents at rest, but interaction data generated while working in private repos. Business and Enterprise tiers excluded.
- **OpenAI shuts down Sora** — https://venturebeat.com/technology/openai-is-shutting-down-sora-its-powerful-ai-video-app — Announced March 24. Sora generated ~$2.1M revenue against hundreds of millions in GPU costs. The Disney $1B partnership is being wound down. Sora 2 remains accessible inside ChatGPT for Plus/Pro subscribers. OpenAI cited refocusing compute toward "world simulation research for robotics."
- **GLM-5.1 open weights confirmed for April 6-7** — Z.ai (Zhipu AI) confirmed in Discord.
- **TeamPCP supply chain campaign continues** — Community discussion on r/MachineLearning about the broader implications of the LiteLLM compromise. Trivy → KICS → LiteLLM → telnyx (4 compromises in 8 days) is prompting serious re-examination of API key management in ML pipelines.
- **MCP crosses 97M monthly SDK downloads** — https://www.digitalapplied.com/blog/march-2026-ai-roundup-month-that-changed-everything — As of February 2026. Every major AI provider now ships MCP-compatible tooling. The protocol's 2026 roadmap focuses on Streamable HTTP transport scaling, Tasks primitive lifecycle, enterprise SSO/audit, and a standard metadata format for registry discovery.

## Research & Papers

- **MSA: Memory Sparse Attention for Efficient End-to-End Memory Model Scaling to 100M Tokens** — https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.23516 — Still the runaway top paper on HuggingFace at 2.27k↑ (up from 2.24k yesterday). Continued momentum.
- **PixelSmile: Toward Fine-Grained Facial Expression Editing** — https://huggingface.co/papers/2603.25728
- **Calibri: Enhancing Diffusion Transformers via Parameter-Efficient Calibration** — https://huggingface.co/papers/2603.24800
- **S2D2: Fast Decoding for Diffusion LLMs via Training-Free Self-Speculation** — https://huggingface.co/papers/2603.25702 — Training-free speculative decoding for diffusion-based language models.
- **PentaNet: Pushing Beyond BitNet with Native Pentanary Quantization** — Extends BitNet's ternary approach to pentanary (5 values), eliminating multiplications entirely.
- **LLM agent access to CS papers improves automated hyperparameter search by 3.2%** — Controlled experiment using Karpathy's autoresearch setup.
- **AVO: Agentic Variation Operators for Autonomous Evolutionary Search** — https://huggingface.co/papers/2603.24517 — LLM agents as variation operators in evolutionary search.

## Agents & Tooling

- **JetBrains Central — open platform for agentic software development** — https://blog.jetbrains.com/blog/2026/03/24/introducing-jetbrains-central-an-open-system-for-agentic-software-development/ — Central acts as the control/execution plane for agent-driven development: governance, execution infrastructure, and semantic context. Supports agents from JetBrains, Anthropic, OpenAI, Google.
- **Dapr Agents v1.0 GA — production-ready agent framework on CNCF** — https://www.cncf.io/announcements/2026/03/23/general-availability-of-dapr-agents-delivers-production-reliability-for-enterprise-ai/ — Python framework for building durable, cloud-native agents. First CNCF-graduated agent framework — brings Kubernetes-native patterns to agent deployment.
- **Kagento — LeetCode for AI Agents** — https://kagento.io — Competitive benchmarking platform where you test your agents against standardized challenges.
- **Toolcast — turn any API into an AI agent tool with one command** — https://github.com/Djsand/toolcast
- **MCP Subagents pattern** — https://cameronwestland.com/mcp-subagents/
- **Visitran — Cursor for data transformations (open source)** — https://github.com/zipstack/visitran

## GitHub Trending

- **obra/superpowers** (2,293★/day) — Agentic skills framework and software development methodology. Composable SKILL.md-based workflows. Works with Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, OpenCode. 120k★. #1 trending on GitHub today.
- **hacksider/Deep-Live-Cam** (1,789★/day) — Real-time face swap. 84k★.
- **onyx-dot-app/onyx** (870★/day) — Open-source AI platform. 19.6k★.
- **datalab-to/chandra** (679★/day) — OCR for complex tables/forms/handwriting. 7.4k★.
- **virattt/dexter** (583★/day) — Autonomous deep financial research agent. 20k★.
- **twentyhq/twenty** (562★/day) — Open-source Salesforce alternative. 42.3k★.
- **SakanaAI/AI-Scientist-v2** (507★/day) — Automated scientific discovery. 3.3k★.
- **agentscope-ai/agentscope** (379★/day) — Production-ready multi-agent framework. 21.5k★.

## Learning & Resources

- **Some uncomfortable truths about AI coding agents** — https://standupforme.app/blog/some-uncomfortable-truths-about-ai-coding-agents/ — 75pts, 87 comments on HN. **High engagement-to-points ratio.** Argues current agent capabilities are overhyped for production use cases. CRITICAL take.
- **Don't Wait for Claude — a workflow for parallel human+AI development** — https://jeapostrophe.github.io/tech/jc-workflow/ — 28pts, 62 comments on HN.
- **AI agents choose tools (and why simpler APIs win)** — https://improbabilityvc.substack.com/p/growth-in-the-age-of-agents — How agent-native API design differs from human-oriented design.
- **How LLMs Feel Language** — https://data-processing.club/cognition/
- **Sloprank — AI-slop scoring for your GitHub repo** — https://sloprank.io/

## Signal

- **TurboQuant adoption is moving at remarkable speed.** Paper → llama.cpp patch → MLX Metal kernels → MacBook Air demo in under a week.
- **Anthropic rate limit backlash is intensifying, not subsiding.** 996↑ open letter, 309↑ quantitative comparison, 157↑ Windows neglect thread.
- **Agent infrastructure is consolidating around real platforms.** JetBrains Central (governance + execution + semantic context), Dapr Agents v1.0 (CNCF, Kubernetes-native), and obra/superpowers (120k★, skills-based methodology) represent converging visions: agents need managed infrastructure, not just prompt chains. The prototype phase is ending.
